page: Microsoft Azure D2pds_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DPDSv5 family, built on the arm64 architecture. It features 2 dedicated physical cores powered by an Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor running at 3.0 GHz, with 1.0 thread per core and no hyperthreading. The hardware configuration includes 8.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core, alongside 77 GB of local SSD storage. The instance runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and supports Premium Storage.

Benchmark data indicates average single-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1822.18 and a single-threaded gzip compression speed of 24.14 GB/sec. However, multi-core and memory-intensive workloads show significant limitations. The stress-ng multi-core score of 3612.77 and Geekbench multi-core score of 2008.0 are categorized as weak. Memory bandwidth is a notable bottleneck, with read-only small block cached bandwidth at 140.69 GB/sec and uncached large block write bandwidth at 15.78 GB/sec. Passmark CPU and memory scores also fall into the poor category, reflecting the architectural limitations of this entry-level ARM tier.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the D2pds_v5 offers a low-cost entry point into ARM-based cloud computing, trading off raw computational power and memory throughput for lower resource density. It is best suited for lightweight workloads that do not require high parallel processing or intensive memory bandwidth, such as basic static web serving, simple text processing, and minor database operations. It is not recommended for memory-bound applications, complex cryptography, or machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D2pds_v5 is an arm64-based general-purpose virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated physical cores on an Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor clocked at 3.0 GHz. Equipped with 8.0 GB of memory and 77 GB of local storage, this instance operates on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Performance benchmarks reveal average single-core capabilities, such as a stress-ng single-core score of 1822.18, but highlight limitations in multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth, where uncached write speeds drop to 15.78 GB/sec. The instance represents a cost-efficient option for low-density workloads, trading off high-throughput performance for architectural efficiency. It is suitable for basic web serving, text processing, and lightweight database operations, but is constrained in memory-intensive or highly parallelized tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D2pds_v5 is an arm64 virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated Ampere Neoverse-N1 cores, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 77 GB of local storage, offering cost-efficient performance for basic workloads.

tagline: An arm64-based virtual machine offering dedicated physical cores and local storage for entry-level, cost-conscious general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 2 dedicated Ampere Neoverse-N1 arm64 physical cores at 3.0 GHz
Includes 8.0 GB of system memory and 77 GB of local SSD storage
Average single-core performance but limited multi-core and memory bandwidth scaling
Qualitatively cost-efficient option for low-density ARM-based deployments
Best suited for static web serving, text processing, and light database tasks
